Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra shows NAV, 30% deduction, and slab tax in one landlord-focused view.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_Motto: Know the taxable rent — not just the rent cheque.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_LearnText: EPF is generally EEE after 5 years of continuous service. Withdrawing earlier can make interest (and sometimes contributions) taxable. This tool uses a simplified interest-portion model for education — not a substitute for Form 31 / EPFO rules.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_FormulaNote: Simplified: ≥5 years → fully exempt; <5 years → assumed interest % of balance is taxable at your slab. Real rules depend on employment continuity, TDS u/s 192A, and Form 15G/15H.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_InterestHint: Used only when service < 5 years. Default 25% is illustrative.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Note_Exempt: Service ≥ 5 years — withdrawal is generally tax-free (EEE).
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Note_Taxable: Service under 5 years — interest portion treated as taxable (simplified).
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Note_Unemployed: Unemployed with <5 years — still showing taxable interest estimate for planning.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_ScenarioSubtitle: Tax if you wait vs withdraw early
Tool_epf_withdrawal_ExampleStep_5: If you wait until 5 years, the same balance is generally fully exempt.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Step_1: Enter balance and years of continuous service.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much of an EPF withdrawal may be taxable before vs after 5 years.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_WhyKnow: Premature EPF withdrawal can create an unexpected tax bill. Knowing the 5-year rule helps timing.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_RealLife: Job change, unemployment claim, retirement planning, Form 31 withdrawal.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Epf_When: To project future EPF balance while contributing.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Tax_When: To see total tax with salary income.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Tax_Example: Add taxable EPF interest to gross.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra highlights the 5-year rule with a clear taxable vs tax-free split.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Motto: Wait for five years when you can — EEE is powerful.
Tool_xirr_LearnText: XIRR is the annualised return when you invest on different dates. Mutual fund statements and SIPs with lumpsums use XIRR. Enter investments as negative amounts and the current/final value as positive.
Tool_xirr_FormulaNote: Solved with Newton–Raphson so that the NPV of all cashflows at rate r equals zero. Needs at least one inflow and one outflow.
Tool_xirr_FinalHint: Current portfolio value or redemption proceeds (positive).
Tool_xirr_VerdictNote: XIRR is about {0}. Absolute profit/loss is {1}.
Tool_xirr_VerdictFail: Could not solve XIRR. Use negative investments and a positive final value with valid dates.
Tool_xirr_ScenarioSubtitle: How XIRR moves if the portfolio ends higher or lower
Tool_xirr_ExampleStep_5: The resulting r is your annualised XIRR.
Tool_xirr_Step_2: Enter final date and positive final value.
Tool_xirr_Info_Abbreviation: XIRR — Extended Internal Rate of Return
Tool_xirr_Info_Purpose: Compute annualised return for irregular dated cashflows.
Tool_xirr_Info_WhyKnow: CAGR assumes one lumpsum. Real portfolios have multiple investments — XIRR is the fair comparison metric.
Tool_xirr_Info_RealLife: MF folio returns, SIP + lumpsum mix, comparing two funds with different cashflow timing.
Tool_xirr_Info_Alt_Cagr_When: Single lumpsum held for whole period.
Tool_xirr_Info_Alt_Sip_When: Regular monthly investments at a fixed return assumption.
Tool_xirr_Info_Alt_Mf_When: Quick lumpsum/SIP future value at assumed CAGR.
Tool_xirr_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra lets you enter 3–5 dated cashflows and solves XIRR with Newton’s method.
Tool_xirr_Info_Motto: Compare returns the way your cash actually moved.
Tool_electricity_bill_LearnText: Most state DISCOMs charge electricity in slabs — the first block of units is cheaper, higher usage costs more per unit. Enter your units and slab rates (defaults are illustrative) to estimate the bill.
Tool_electricity_bill_FormulaNote: Illustrative only. Real bills add duty, fuel surcharge, and state-specific slabs. Check your DISCOM tariff card.
Tool_electricity_bill_Slab1Hint: First column: upper unit limit. Second: rate ₹/unit.
Tool_electricity_bill_Slab2Hint: Second slab ends at this unit limit.
Tool_electricity_bill_Slab3Hint: All units above slab 2 at this rate.
Tool_electricity_bill_Step_3: Charge each band of units at its rate.
Tool_electricity_bill_Step_4: Add fixed charge for total bill.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Purpose: Estimate a domestic electricity bill using simple multi-slab rates.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_WhyKnow: Understanding slabs shows why cutting peak usage saves more than cutting the first cheap units.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_RealLife: Monthly household bill check, AC season planning, comparing tariff cards.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Alt_Budget_Wh: To fit the bill into overall expenses.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Alt_Inflation: To see how rising tariffs erode purchasing power.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Alt_Goal_When: Saving the difference from lower usage.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Alt_Goal_Exam: Redirect savings to an emergency fund.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra shows a clear per-slab breakdown plus fixed charge.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Motto: The last units cost the most — use slabs to your advantage.
Tool_freelancer_tax_LearnText: Freelancers and professionals can compute profit as receipts minus expenses, or opt for presumptive taxation under Section 44ADA (typically 50% of gross receipts treated as income). This tool compares both and estimates tax at your marginal slab.
Tool_freelancer_tax_FormulaNote: 44ADA eligibility and turnover limits change with Budget rules. Educational estimate only — GST, advance tax, and TDS credits not modelled.
Tool_freelancer_tax_44ADAHint: When checked, taxable profit = 50% of receipts.
Tool_freelancer_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: Which method shows lower taxable profit?
Tool_freelancer_tax_ExampleStep_5: Compare both before choosing a method (eligibility permitting).
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate freelancer tax under books profit or Section 44ADA.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Presumptive tax can cut compliance and sometimes tax — but only if you qualify and expenses are not very high.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_RealLife: Consultants, designers, doctors, freelancers comparing ITR methods.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Alt_Tax_When: Full regime comparison with other income.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Alt_Tds_When: Clients deduct TDS on professional fees.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra puts 44ADA and books profit side-by-side with a slab tax estimate.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Motto: Pick the method that fits your expenses — and the law.
Tool_gift_tax_LearnText: Under Section 56(2)(x), gifts from specified relatives are generally exempt. From non-relatives, if the aggregate gift exceeds ₹50,000 in a year, the entire amount can be taxed as income from other sources. This tool estimates tax at your slab.
Tool_gift_tax_FormulaNote: Simplified cash-gift model. Property, shares, and wedding gifts have special rules. Educational only.
Tool_gift_tax_RelativeHint: Parents, spouse, siblings, and certain in-laws are typically “specified relatives”.
Tool_gift_tax_Verdict_Relative: Gift from a specified relative is generally fully exempt.
Tool_gift_tax_Verdict_Taxable: Non-relative gift above the threshold — estimated tax {0} at {1}.
Tool_gift_tax_Verdict_Exempt: Non-relative gift at or below {0} — not taxable under this simplified rule.
Tool_gift_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: How relationship and amount change tax
Tool_gift_tax_ExampleTitle: Worked example: ₹2 lakh from a friend
Tool_gift_tax_ExampleStep_4: If the same gift were from a parent, tax would be ₹0.
Tool_gift_tax_ExampleStep_5: If the gift were ₹50,000 or less from a friend, tax would also be ₹0 (simplified).
Tool_gift_tax_Step_3: If non-relative and > ₹50k → full amount taxable.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Purpose: Check whether a gift is taxable and estimate tax at your slab.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Large gifts from friends or distant acquaintances can create a surprise tax liability.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_RealLife: Cash gifts, festival money from non-relatives, family transfers vs friend transfers.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Alt_Tax_When: To include gift income in full ITR.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Alt_80c_When: For deduction planning on other investments.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra makes the relative vs ₹50k rule obvious with a clear tax estimate.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Motto: Know who gave the gift — it can change the tax entirely.
Tool_car_insurance_LearnText: Own-damage (OD) premium is often quoted as a percentage of Insured Declared Value (IDV). Third-party (TP) liability is a flat IRDAI-linked amount by vehicle class. This tool multiplies IDV by your OD rate and adds a TP estimate — educational only, not an insurer quote. NCB and GST are not modelled here.
Tool_car_insurance_FormulaNote: Actual premiums depend on make/model, city, NCB, add-ons, and underwriting. Confirm with your insurer.
Tool_car_insurance_IdvHint: Approximate market value of the car for insurance.
Tool_car_insurance_OdRateHint: Typical OD rates are often around 1–3% before NCB; enter your quote rate.
Tool_car_insurance_TpHint: Flat TP liability — varies by cubic capacity; default is illustrative.
Tool_car_insurance_ScenarioSubtitle: Lower OD rate vs higher IDV
Tool_car_insurance_ExampleStep_5: NCB can cut OD further — check your renewal quote.
Tool_car_insurance_Step_2: Enter OD rate as % of IDV.
Tool_car_insurance_Step_3: Add a flat third-party premium estimate.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Purpose: Roughly estimate comprehensive premium from IDV and OD rate plus TP.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_WhyKnow: IDV and OD rate drive most of the own-damage cost; TP is largely fixed by class.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_RealLife: Comparing renewal quotes or budgeting for a new car policy.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Alt_Health_When: When estimating family health cover need.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Alt_Health_Examp: Sum insured by age and city tier.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra keeps OD + TP transparent from IDV and rate — no opaque quote black box.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Motto: Know your OD math before you renew.
Tool_rent_agreement_LearnText: Rental agreements often attract stamp duty (and sometimes registration) as a percentage of total rent over the tenure. Security deposit is usually a multiple of monthly rent. This tool uses simplified state rates for education — verify with your local stamp office or lawyer.
Tool_rent_agreement_FormulaNote: Rates and whether registration is mandatory vary by state and tenure (e.g. 11 vs 12 months). Figures are illustrative.
Tool_rent_agreement_MonthsHint: Many leases use 11 months to avoid registration in some states.
Tool_rent_agreement_DepositHint: Commonly 1–6 months depending on city and landlord.
Tool_rent_agreement_ScenarioSubtitle: 11 months vs 12 months vs more deposit
Tool_rent_agreement_Step_1: Enter monthly rent and tenure months.
Tool_rent_agreement_Step_3: Set security deposit in months of rent.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_Purpose: Estimate stamp/registration and security deposit for a rental lease.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_WhyKnow: Upfront cash for stamp and deposit can surprise tenants at move-in.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_RealLife: Signing a new flat lease and budgeting move-in costs.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_Alt_Hra_When: For tax exemption on rent paid.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra combines simplified state stamp rates with deposit months in one upfront view.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_Motto: Budget stamp and deposit before you sign.
Tool_compound_interest_LearnText: Compound interest grows principal by applying rate on accumulated interest. Frequency (annual, quarterly, monthly, daily) changes the effective return. Rule of 72 roughly estimates years to double. Same math as FinYatra’s core compound tool, under the India /calc route.
Tool_compound_interest_FormulaNote: Uses the standard compoundInterest(P, r, t, n) formula. Rates are nominal annual.
Tool_compound_interest_ScenarioSubtitle: Annual vs your frequency vs quarterly
Tool_compound_interest_ExampleTitle: Worked example: ₹1 lakh at 8% for 10 years
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Purpose: Project maturity with different compounding frequencies.
Tool_compound_interest_Info_WhyKnow: More frequent compounding raises effective yield for the same nominal rate.
Tool_compound_interest_Info_RealLife: Comparing FD offers or savings growth assumptions.
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Goal_2: Compare annual vs monthly vs quarterly
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Goal_3: Use Rule of 72 for doubling time
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Alt_Fd_When: For bank FD with TDS options.
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Differentiat: India /calc compound interest with frequency scenarios and year table — same core formula.
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Motto: Frequency matters as much as the rate.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_LearnText: PPF allows a loan roughly from the 3rd to 6th financial year, and partial withdrawal from the 7th year onward. Official caps use balances at end of specific prior years; this tool simplifies to ~25% (loan) or ~50% (withdrawal) of your current balance for education.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_FormulaNote: Simplified fractions — your bank/PO uses prior-year closing balances. Premature closure has separate rules and penalties.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_YearHint: 1 = first FY of the account. Partial withdrawal typically from year 7.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Note_TooEarly: Too early — loan from year 3; partial withdrawal from year 7.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Note_LoanClosed: Loan facility typically ends after year 6 — use partial withdrawal instead.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Note_Mature: After maturity (15+ years), full balance may be withdrawable (extension rules apply).
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_ScenarioSubtitle: Loan vs withdrawal vs later year
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_ExampleStep_3: Loan mode at year 8 is typically closed.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_ExampleStep_4: At year 5, loan ≈ 25% = ₹1,25,000; withdrawal not yet allowed.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_ExampleStep_5: Confirm exact limit with your bank using prior-year balances.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Step_1: Enter current account year and balance.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Step_3: Apply simplified year windows and fractions.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much you can borrow or partially withdraw from PPF.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_WhyKnow: Liquidity rules are year-gated; withdrawing early can be blocked or capped.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_RealLife: Planning a home down payment or education expense from PPF.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_Goal_2: Know year windows for loan vs withdrawal
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra clarifies loan vs withdrawal year gates with a simple max estimate.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_Motto: Know the year gate before you need the cash.
Tool_mf_overlap_LearnText: Two equity funds can look diversified but hold the same large-cap names. Paste top holdings as symbols (optional :weight). If all holdings have weights, overlap is the sum of min(weightA, weightB); otherwise it is shared symbols ÷ union count.
Tool_mf_overlap_FormulaNote: Educational only — use latest factsheets. High overlap may mean less true diversification.
Tool_mf_overlap_HoldingsHint: Comma-separated: RELIANCE:8, TCS:6, INFY (weights optional, % of portfolio).
Tool_mf_overlap_Result_MethodFootnote: Weighted if every holding has a weight
Tool_mf_overlap_ScenarioSubtitle: Count method vs weighted vs half of Fund A
Tool_mf_overlap_ExampleStep_1: Parse both lists into symbols (and weights if given).
Tool_mf_overlap_ExampleStep_3: If weighted: sum min(wA, wB) for each shared name.
Tool_mf_overlap_ExampleStep_5: High overlap → consider different styles or categories.
Tool_mf_overlap_Step_4: Review shared names and method used.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_Purpose: Measure how much two funds share the same stocks.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_WhyKnow: Owning two funds with 60%+ overlap may not diversify risk.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_RealLife: Picking a second equity fund for a SIP portfolio.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra accepts paste-friendly holdings with optional weights for a quick pairwise score.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_Motto: Diversify holdings, not just fund names.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_LearnText: This is NOT a live government status API. It does not verify your PAN. Use it only to understand illustrative late-linking fees (historically around ₹500 / ₹1,000) and why an unlinked PAN can become inoperative for many transactions. Always check status on the Income Tax e-filing portal.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_FormulaNote: Penalty amounts are illustrative fixed figures for education — confirm current rules on incometax.gov.in.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_StatusHint: Self-reported only — we never call a government API.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_DeadlineHint: Historical education: lower vs higher fixed late fee.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Disclaimer: FinYatra does not check real PAN–Aadhaar status. Use the Income Tax Department portal to link or verify.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Consequence_Ok: If already linked, no late-link fee applies in this model.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Consequence_Inoper: Unlinked PAN may become inoperative — affecting TDS credit, refunds, and some high-value transactions until linked.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Consequence_Unknow: Check your status on the e-filing portal before assuming a fee.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_ScenarioSubtitle: Lower fee vs higher fee vs already linked
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_ExampleTitle: Worked example: not linked, after deadline
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_ExampleStep_2: Assume after illustrative deadline → higher fee.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_ExampleStep_5: Verify and link only on the official IT portal.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Step_3: If not linked, show fixed educational fee.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Step_4: Read inoperative-PAN consequences; verify on IT portal.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_Purpose: Explain late-link fees and inoperative PAN risks without a live API.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_WhyKnow: An inoperative PAN can block refunds and complicate TDS — link early on the official site.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_RealLife: Someone unsure if they paid the late fee or need to link.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_Goal_3: Know this is not a live status check
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra is explicitly educational — no fake “status API”, only fee context and consequences.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_Motto: Link on the official portal — we only explain the stakes.

Tool_house_property_income_Info_Purpose: Estimate net annual value and tax on let-out / house property income.
Tool_house_property_income_Info_WhyKnow: Rental income is taxed after municipal tax, a flat 30% deduction, and interest — knowing NAV helps plan.
Tool_house_property_income_Info_RealLife: Landlords computing taxable rental income for ITR.
Tool_house_property_income_Info_Alt_Rent: When you want a rent-focused walkthrough.
Tool_house_property_income_Info_Alt_Tax_: For full-year liability with salary and other heads.
Tool_house_property_income_Info_Differen: FinYatra shows GAV → municipal → 30% → interest → NAV and slab tax in one view.
Tool_house_property_income_Info_Motto: From rent to NAV — then tax.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_LearnText: Enter unpaid tax, months of delay, and how many advance-tax instalments were short. See a rough 234A, 234B, and 234C interest total.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_FormulaNote: Educational 1%/month sketch. Exact 234B/C rules, assessed tax, and dates need Form computation — confirm with a CA.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_MonthsHint: Used for both 234A (late filing) and 234B (advance tax) in this simplified tool.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_InstalmentsHin: How many quarterly advance-tax instalments were short.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_ScenarioSubtit: Same tax due, different months late
Tool_interest_penalty_234_ExampleStep_5: Total interest ≈ ₹6,250 — file and pay early to cut months.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_Step_4: Sum simplified A + B + C interest.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_Info_Purpose: Roughly estimate late-filing and advance-tax interest.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_Info_WhyKnow: Delay multiplies cost at ~1% per month — knowing the ballpark helps prioritise payment.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_Info_RealLife: ITR filed after due date or advance tax missed.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_Info_Alt_Tax_W: To find the tax due before interest.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_Info_Alt_Tds_W: When TDS already covers most liability.
Tool_interest_penalty_234_Info_Different: FinYatra stacks simplified 234A, 234B, and 234C in one delay-cost view.
Tool_agricultural_income_LearnText: Enter agricultural income and other taxable income. See a simplified partial-integration estimate: agri stays exempt but can push other income into higher slabs.
Tool_agricultural_income_FormulaNote: Educational aggregation sketch with simplified new-regime-style slabs. Exact Rule and old/new regime differences need a CA.
Tool_agricultural_income_OtherHint: Salary, business, etc. — non-agricultural taxable income.
Tool_agricultural_income_ExampleStep_2: Tax on other alone uses simplified slabs.
Tool_agricultural_income_ExampleStep_3: Tax on total minus tax on (agri + basic exemption) ≈ rate-bump tax.
Tool_agricultural_income_ExampleStep_4: Agri itself remains exempt — only the slab effect is taxed.
Tool_agricultural_income_ExampleStep_5: Higher agri can increase the bump even though agri is exempt.
Tool_agricultural_income_Step_2: Compute tax on combined income (simplified slabs).
Tool_agricultural_income_Step_3: Subtract tax on agri + basic exemption.
Tool_agricultural_income_Step_4: Difference ≈ extra tax from rate bump.
Tool_agricultural_income_Info_Purpose: Show how exempt agri income can still raise tax on other income.
Tool_agricultural_income_Info_WhyKnow: Agri is exempt but used for rate purposes — ignoring it understates tax.
Tool_agricultural_income_Info_RealLife: Farmers or landowners with salary/business income.
Tool_agricultural_income_Info_Alt_Tax_Wh: For full-year liability without agri focus.
Tool_agricultural_income_Info_Alt_Budget: For cash-flow, not tax rate bump.
Tool_agricultural_income_Info_Alt_Goal_W: Saving toward a target from mixed income.
Tool_agricultural_income_Info_Differenti: FinYatra isolates the partial-integration rate bump from exempt agri income.
Tool_agricultural_income_Info_Motto: Exempt — but not ignored for rates.
Tool_bonus_LearnText: Enter basic pay and either a bonus percentage (e.g. statutory 8.33–20%) or a fixed amount. See bonus payable and a rough tax tip at your slab.
Tool_bonus_FormulaNote: Educational. Payment of Bonus Act ceilings and employer rules vary — confirm with HR/payroll.
Tool_bonus_ExampleStep_4: At 20%, bonus would be ₹8,000 with a higher tax tip.
Tool_bonus_ExampleStep_5: Fixed-amount mode skips the % and uses your entered bonus.
Tool_bonus_Step_2: Choose % of basic or fixed amount.
Tool_bonus_Info_Purpose: Estimate bonus from basic and see a rough tax tip.
Tool_bonus_Info_WhyKnow: Bonus is taxable salary — knowing net after tax helps planning.
Tool_bonus_Info_Alt_Salary_When: For full CTC breakdown, not just bonus.
Tool_bonus_Info_Alt_Tax_When: When bonus is part of annual income.
Tool_bonus_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra lets you switch % vs amount and shows a slab tax tip beside the bonus.
Tool_bonus_Info_Motto: Know the bonus — and the tax tip.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_LearnText: Enter arrears received this year, how many prior years they relate to, and your current slab. See a rough lump-sum tax vs spread tax and estimated 89(1) relief.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_FormulaNote: Educational only. Real Rule 21A / Form 10E uses year-wise tax recomputation — file Form 10E and confirm with a CA.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_YearsHint: How many prior years the arrears belong to (for spread).
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_ExampleStep_5: Claim via Form 10E — this tool is a teaching sketch only.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_Step_2: Set how many prior years they relate to.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_Step_3: Compare tax at current slab vs lower prior slab.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_Info_Purpose: Roughly estimate relief when salary arrears are taxed in one year.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_Info_WhyKnow: Lumping arrears can spike tax; 89(1) may reduce the extra burden.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_Info_RealLife: DA / pay revision arrears paid in a later year.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_Info_Alt_Sala: For regular monthly pay, not arrears.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_Info_Alt_Da_W: To compute DA arrears amount first.
Tool_salary_arrears_relief_Info_Differen: FinYatra contrasts lump vs spread tax for a quick 89(1) teaching estimate.
Tool_pay_commission_LearnText: Enter current basic pay and a fitment factor (7th CPC used ~2.57). See revised basic and the increase — useful for CPC-style estimates.
Tool_pay_commission_FormulaNote: Educational fitment only. Pay matrix levels, DA/HRA/TA, and any future CPC differ — not an official pay slip.
Tool_pay_commission_FitmentHint: 7th CPC fitment was about 2.57; try nearby values to compare.
Tool_pay_commission_ExampleStep_5: DA/HRA still apply on revised basic in real pay slips.
Tool_pay_commission_Info_Purpose: Estimate revised basic after a CPC-style fitment.
Tool_pay_commission_Info_WhyKnow: Fitment multiplies basic before DA/HRA — a quick revised-pay ballpark.
Tool_pay_commission_Info_Alt_Da_When: For dearness allowance on existing basic.
Tool_pay_commission_Info_Alt_Salary_When: For private-sector CTC, not CPC matrix.
Tool_pay_commission_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra focuses on basic × fitment for a clear CPC-style revised basic.
Tool_gst_annual_return_LearnText: Enter taxable outward supplies, an effective GST rate, and tax already paid. Compare liability with paid amount to see excess, shortfall, or balance — educational only, not GSTR-9 filing.
Tool_gst_annual_return_FormulaNote: Simplified reconciliation. ITC splits, late fees, and portal tables are not modelled. Confirm with a GST practitioner.
Tool_gst_annual_return_RateHint: Illustrative blended rate on outward supplies (e.g. 18%).
Tool_gst_annual_return_ScenarioSubtitle: Same outward supplies, different tax paid
Tool_gst_annual_return_ExampleStep_5: This is an educational check only — not a substitute for GSTR-9.
Tool_gst_annual_return_Step_1: Enter taxable outward supplies for the year.
Tool_gst_annual_return_Info_Purpose: Compare tax paid with a simple liability on outward supplies.
Tool_gst_annual_return_Info_WhyKnow: Annual return season often needs a quick paid-vs-liability sense-check before filing.
Tool_gst_annual_return_Info_RealLife: Traders reconciling GSTR-3B payments against yearly outward supplies.
Tool_gst_annual_return_Info_Goal_1: Estimate GST liability on outward supplies
Tool_gst_annual_return_Info_Alt_Freelanc: When you also track income tax on receipts.
Tool_gst_annual_return_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra shows paid vs liability difference without pretending to file GSTR-9.
Tool_gst_annual_return_Info_Motto: Paid vs liability — before you file.
Tool_epfo_passbook_LearnText: Enter opening balance, combined monthly EPF contribution, months, and rate. See projected closing, total contributions, and interest — a simplified passbook projection.
Tool_epfo_passbook_FormulaNote: Monthly interest approximation. Official EPFO credits interest yearly with specific rules — educational only.
Tool_epfo_passbook_MonthlyHint: Employee + employer EPF share combined (not EPS).
Tool_epfo_passbook_ExampleStep_2: Each month: add contribution, then interest at rate/12.
Tool_epfo_passbook_ExampleStep_5: Longer tenure compounds more interest on a growing balance.
Tool_epfo_passbook_Info_Purpose: Estimate how your EPF balance may grow over coming months.
Tool_epfo_passbook_Info_WhyKnow: Helps plan withdrawals, loans, or retirement corpus from a passbook view.
Tool_epfo_passbook_Info_RealLife: Salaried employees checking UAN passbook growth.
Tool_epfo_passbook_Info_Alt_Epf_When: For multi-year corpus from basic wages.
Tool_epfo_passbook_Info_Alt_Nps_When: For NPS Tier-I corpus, not EPF.
Tool_epfo_passbook_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra gives a simple opening + months passbook projection alongside full EPF tools.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_LearnText: Enter LTA claimed, exempt limit (eligible fare), and trips used in the block. See exempt vs taxable amount under simplified Section 10(5) rules.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_FormulaNote: Actual exemption depends on fare proofs and block-year rules. Up to 2 journeys in a 4-year block (illustrative).
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_ExemptHint: Usually eligible travel fare for the journey(s).
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_TripsHint: Typically up to {0} trips in a {1}-year block.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_ExampleStep_: If trips exceed the block limit, exemption may be nil in this simplified model.
Keep fare proofs — employers and IT may ask for them.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_Step_3: Set trips used in the 4-year block.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much LTA is exempt vs taxable.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_Info_WhyKnow: Over-claiming without fare support can create taxable salary.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_Info_RealLif: Employees claiming LTA reimbursement for domestic travel.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_Info_Alt_Hra: For house rent exemption, not travel.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_Info_Differe: Same Sec 10(5) logic as FinYatra LTA — dedicated leave-travel-allowance route.
Tool_leave_travel_allowance_Info_Motto: Claim smart — keep the exempt part.
Tool_post_office_savings_LearnText: Enter deposit, annual rate, years, and compounding frequency. See maturity and total interest using A = P(1+r/n)^(n×t).
Tool_post_office_savings_FormulaNote: Illustrative. POSA and other schemes have specific rates and rules — confirm with India Post / NSI.
Tool_post_office_savings_RateHint: POSA is often near ~4% — enter the rate you want to model.
Tool_post_office_savings_ScenarioSubtitl: Same deposit and rate, different frequency
Tool_post_office_savings_ExampleTitle: Worked example: ₹1 lakh at 4% for 5 years
Tool_post_office_savings_ExampleStep_4: Monthly compounding earns slightly more than annual.
Tool_post_office_savings_ExampleStep_5: Compare with PO TD / MIS for different goals.
Tool_post_office_savings_Info_Abbreviati: PO savings — compound interest on deposit
Tool_post_office_savings_Info_Purpose: Estimate interest on a Post Office-style savings deposit.
Tool_post_office_savings_Info_WhyKnow: Helps compare POSA-style returns with bank savings or TD.
Tool_post_office_savings_Info_RealLife: Small deposits parked in Post Office savings.
Tool_post_office_savings_Info_Differenti: FinYatra models PO savings compounding next to TD, MIS, and PPF tools.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_LearnText: Enter basic pay, DA%, HRA%, other allowances, and total deductions. See DA, HRA, gross, and net — a simplified central government salary slip.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_FormulaNot: Illustrative. Actual slips include NPS, CGEGIS, income tax, and city-class HRA — confirm with your DDO / payslip.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_HraHint: Often 30% / 20% / 10% by city class — enter your rate.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Deductions: NPS, CGEGIS, income tax, and other recoveries combined.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_ExampleSte: Gross = basic + DA + HRA + other allowances.
A DA hike raises both gross and net if deductions stay fixed.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Step_4: Gross = sum of earnings; net = gross − deductions.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_Abbre: Central govt payslip — basic + DA + HRA
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_Purpo: Estimate gross and net from a simplified 7th CPC-style slip.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_WhyKn: DA revisions and HRA city class change take-home quickly.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_RealL: Central government employees checking monthly in-hand.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_Diffe: FinYatra builds a central-govt style slip with DA and HRA percentages in one view.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_LearnText: Enter one annual deposit (capped at ₹1.5 lakh) and rates. Compare SSY (15 deposit years, mature at 21) with PPF (15 years) side by side.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_FormulaNote: SSY is for a girl child; PPF is open to individuals. Rates change quarterly — educational comparison only.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_DepositHint: Max ₹1.5 lakh per year for both schemes (shared cap in this tool).
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_2: SSY: deposit 15 years, interest continues to year 21 at 8.2%.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_3: PPF: 15 years at 7.1% with annual compounding.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_4: Gap = SSY maturity − PPF maturity (often favours SSY on rate + time).
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_5: Eligibility differs — SSY needs a girl child account.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Step_1: Enter the same annual deposit for both.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Step_3: Project SSY to year 21 and PPF to year 15.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Info_Purpose: Compare Sukanya Samriddhi and PPF for the same yearly deposit.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Info_WhyKnow: Both are EEE small-savings favourites — rate and tenure differ.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Info_RealLife: Parents choosing between SSY and PPF for long-term goals.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra puts SSY and PPF maturity on one screen for the same deposit.
Tool_pension_commutation_LearnText: Commutation lets you take a portion of pension as a one-time lump sum. Enter monthly pension, commutation % (max 40%), and a commutation factor (years of purchase). The tool shows lump sum, reduced monthly pension, and a simple break-even.
Tool_pension_commutation_FormulaNote: Simplified educational model. Official factors vary by age and service rules; restoration after ~15 years is not modelled here.
Tool_pension_commutation_PercentHint: Typically up to 40% of pension can be commuted.
Tool_pension_commutation_FactorHint: Years of purchase — ~9.81 is a common illustrative age-60 factor.
Tool_pension_commutation_Step_4: See lump sum, reduced pension, and break-even.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Abbreviati: Pension commutation — lump sum vs reduced pension
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Purpose: Estimate lump sum and reduced pension when commuting a share of pension.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_WhyKnow: Commutation trades lifelong pension for cash now — break-even helps decide.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_RealLife: Central/state pensioners choosing commutation at retirement.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Goal_2: See lump sum vs reduced pension
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Nps_Wh: For contribution and corpus planning before annuity.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Annuit: For pension from a retirement corpus.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Tax_Wh: For tax on pension / lump sum context.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Differenti: FinYatra shows lump sum, reduced pension, and break-even in one view.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Motto: Cash now vs pension forever — know the trade-off.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_LearnText: Under the old regime, Section 24(b) allows interest deduction (₹2L cap if self-occupied) and Section 80C allows principal repayment up to ₹1.5L. Enter first-year interest and principal, occupancy, regime, and slab to estimate tax saving.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_FormulaNote: Educational. New regime generally disallows these deductions. Joint-loan higher limits are not auto-modelled — enter your share of interest/principal.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_RegimeHint: New regime: 24(b)/80C home-loan benefits typically not available (simplified).
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_ScenarioSubti: Self-occupied vs let-out vs new regime
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_ExampleStep_1: Interest ₹2,50,000; principal ₹1,80,000; self-occupied; old regime; 30% slab.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Purpose: Estimate old-regime tax saving from home-loan interest and principal.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_WhyKnow: Self-occupied interest is capped; let-out interest is often fully deductible (simplified).
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_RealLife: First home loan, comparing old vs new regime, joint ownership share.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_Home: To derive EMI and first-year split from loan terms.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_80c_: To plan the full ₹1.5L basket including ELSS/PPF.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_Tax_: For overall old vs new regime compare.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Differen: FinYatra isolates 24(b) and 80C with old vs new regime contrast.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Motto: Interest and principal — two deductions, one home loan.
Tool_ltcg_LearnText: Long-term capital gains (LTCG) apply after the holding threshold — 12 months for equity, 24 months for property (simplified). Equity gets a ₹1.25L exemption then 12.5%; property is taxed at 12.5% with optional 54/54EC exemption.
Tool_ltcg_FormulaNote: Illustrative post-Budget rates. Indexation for property is not auto-applied here — enter indexed cost as buy price if needed.
Tool_ltcg_ExemptionHint: Optional amount that reduces taxable LTCG.
Tool_ltcg_Result_BelowThreshold: Holding below LTCG threshold — LTCG math shown for reference
Tool_ltcg_Step_2: Enter buy, sell, and holding months.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Purpose: Estimate long-term capital gains tax on equity or property.
Tool_ltcg_Info_WhyKnow: Holding period and the ₹1.25L equity exemption change the tax bill.
Tool_ltcg_Info_RealLife: Selling stocks after a year, property sale with 54EC bonds.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Alt_Cg_When: When you need STCG and LTCG in one tool.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Alt_Elss_When: For tax-saving equity with LTCG on exit.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra focuses on LTCG with equity exemption and 54/54EC inputs.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Motto: Hold long enough — then tax the gain, not the whole sale.
Tool_stcg_LearnText: Short-term capital gains (STCG) apply before the long-term threshold. Listed equity STCG is often 20%; property STCG is taxed at your slab. Compare selling now vs waiting for LTCG.
Tool_stcg_FormulaNote: Illustrative rates. Waiting assumes the same sell price — markets can move.
Tool_stcg_HoldingHint: STCG if equity < 12 months or property < 24 months.
Tool_stcg_ScenarioSubtitle: STCG today vs LTCG after threshold
Tool_stcg_ExampleStep_4: If waited for LTCG: exempt ₹1.25L, taxable ₹25,000 × 12.5%.
Tool_stcg_ExampleStep_5: Compare tax saved by waiting (price assumed unchanged).
Tool_stcg_Step_2: Enter buy, sell, holding, and slab.
Tool_stcg_Step_3: See STCG tax at 20% or slab.
Tool_stcg_Info_Purpose: Estimate short-term capital gains tax and wait-vs-sell impact.
Tool_stcg_Info_WhyKnow: A few months of holding can change equity tax from 20% STCG to 12.5% LTCG.
Tool_stcg_Info_RealLife: Booking profits early, property flip, year-end tax planning.
Tool_stcg_Info_Alt_Cg_When: For both STCG and LTCG together.
Tool_stcg_Info_Alt_Elss_When: For lock-in equity that exits as LTCG.
Tool_stcg_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra highlights sell-now STCG vs wait-for-LTCG side by side.
Tool_stcg_Info_Motto: Sometimes waiting a few months is the cheapest tax tip.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_LearnText: Tax-loss harvesting uses realised losses to offset gains. Simplified India rules: STCL can set off STCG then LTCG; LTCL can set off only LTCG. Unused losses may be carried forward (shown as remaining).
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_FormulaNote: Educational set-off order. India has no US-style wash-sale rule, but other conditions apply. Confirm with a CA.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_ExampleStep_1: STCL ₹1.5L offsets STCG ₹2L → net STCG ₹50k; STCL left = 0.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_ExampleStep_4: Compare with tax if no losses were booked.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Step_4: See net gains and tax saved.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Purpose: Estimate how realised losses reduce taxable capital gains.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_WhyKnow: Year-end harvesting can cut tax without changing long-term allocation much.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_RealLife: Booking losers before 31 Mar, rebalancing with tax awareness.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Goal_3: See tax saved vs no harvest
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Differenti: FinYatra applies simplified STCL/LTCL set-off and shows tax saved clearly.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Motto: Harvest losses with rules — not randomly.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_LearnText: Advance tax is paid in instalments: 15% by 15 Jun, 45% by 15 Sep, 75% by 15 Dec, and 100% by 15 Mar. Enter estimated tax and amount already paid to see remaining tax, shortfall for the current milestone, and the full calendar.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_FormulaNote: Educational schedule. 234C interest on shortfall is not computed here — use the interest/penalty tool for that. Presumptive taxpayers may have different timing.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_PaidHint: Advance tax + TDS credited toward liability (simplified).
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_CalendarSubtit: Cumulative % and amount due by each date
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_ExampleStep_5: Use the calendar for Jun/Sep/Dec/Mar milestones.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Step_1: Enter estimated tax and amount paid.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Abbreviat: Advance tax due dates — quarterly calendar
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Purpose: Show how much advance tax is due by each quarterly date.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_WhyKnow: Missing a milestone can attract 234C interest.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_RealLife: Self-employed quarterly payments, freelancers tracking TDS + advance.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Goal_1: Enter estimated tax and paid amount
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Goal_2: See shortfall for current due date
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Alt_Tds_W: When TDS already covers part of tax.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Alt_Advan: For installment amount focus with same schedule.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Different: FinYatra centres the due-date calendar with remaining-to-milestone amounts.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Motto: Know the date — then pay the percent.
Tool_ups_LearnText: Enter basic pay, service years, and contribution rates. See UPS assured pension (50% at 25 years, pro-rata below) versus a simplified NPS corpus/pension from the same contributions.
Tool_ups_FormulaNote: Educational only. Official UPS rules, DA, family pension, and NPS annuity options differ — confirm with your cadre / CA.
Tool_ups_YearsHint: Full 50% assured pension typically needs 25 years (simplified).
Tool_ups_ExampleStep_3: Monthly contrib = 80,000 × 28.5% = ₹22,800 invested in NPS comparison.
Tool_ups_ExampleStep_4: NPS corpus grows for 25 years; pension ≈ corpus × annuity%/12.
Tool_ups_ExampleStep_5: Gap = UPS pension − NPS pension (educational comparison only).
Tool_ups_Step_1: Enter monthly basic and years of service.
Tool_ups_Step_4: Compare with NPS SIP corpus → annuity pension.
Tool_ups_Info_Purpose: Estimate assured UPS pension and compare with a simplified NPS path.
Tool_ups_Info_WhyKnow: Central govt employees weighing UPS vs NPS need a clear pension gap view.
Tool_ups_Info_RealLife: Choosing or understanding UPS assured payout versus market-linked NPS.
Tool_ups_Info_Alt_Nps_When: For full Tier-I corpus and annuity split.
Tool_ups_Info_Alt_Epf_When: For EPF corpus, not pension scheme choice.
Tool_ups_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows UPS assured % vs a same-contribution NPS estimate in one view.
Tool_ups_Info_Motto: Assured vs market — see the gap.
Tool_itr_form_LearnText: Tick the income sources that apply. We suggest ITR-1, 2, 3, or 4 using simplified rules — always confirm on the Income Tax portal before filing.
Tool_itr_form_FormulaNote: Educational selector only. Defective returns, schedules, and edge cases need a CA or the official utility.

Tool_itr_form_Step_1: Tick all income sources that apply.

Tool_itr_form_Info_RealLife: Salaried filers checking Sahaj vs ITR-2 before e-filing.

Tool_gst_itc_LearnText: Enter outward GST liability and eligible ITC. See net cash payable or leftover credit — simplified, not GSTR-2B / Rule 42 reconciliation.

Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_LearnText: Enter annual housing cost (rent or EMI proxy), basic, HRA received, metro status, interest share of loan cost, and tax slab. Compare HRA exemption tax saving vs Section 24(b)+80C home-loan tax saving (old regime, educational).

Tool_income_tax_notice_Deadline_reassess: File return / reply as directed — seek CA help
Tool_income_tax_notice_Deadline_demand: Pay or contest within the notice timeline
Tool_income_tax_notice_Deadline_adjustme: Check refund adjustment; object if incorrect
Tool_income_tax_notice_Deadline_generic: Identify section and respond by the stated date
Tool_income_tax_notice_Meaning_143_1: Processing intimation after return filing — often tax/refund computation vs your ITR.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Meaning_143_2: Scrutiny selection — department may ask for documents and explanations.

Tool_income_tax_notice_Meaning_156: Demand notice for tax, interest, or penalty payable.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Meaning_245: Proposed set-off of refund against outstanding demand.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Meaning_other: Confirm the exact section on the letter and use e-filing help / a CA.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_verify_inti: Compare intimation with Form 16 / AIS / ITR
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_match_form1: Match TDS credits in Form 26AS / AIS
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_check_refun: Check refund or demand on the portal
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_respond_por: Respond or rectify via e-filing if needed
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_check_reaso: Ask for / review reasons for reopening
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_file_return: File return in response if required
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_verify_dema: Verify demand computation line by line
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_pay_or_rect: Pay via challan or seek rectification
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_update_chal: Update challan / CIN on the portal
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_check_adjus: Check which demand is being adjusted
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_object_if_w: File objection if adjustment is wrong
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_identify_se: Identify the exact section and DIN
Tool_income_tax_notice_Check_keep_copies: Keep PDF copies of notice and replies
Tool_income_tax_notice_ExampleStep_3: Checklist: match Form 16 / AIS, verify TDS, check portal.
Tool_income_tax_notice_ExampleStep_4: If mismatch, respond or seek rectification.
Tool_income_tax_notice_ExampleStep_5: Compare with scrutiny / demand notice scenarios.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Step_2: Read the meaning and deadline note.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Info_Purpose: Understand common income-tax notice types and next steps.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Info_WhyKnow: Wrong or late responses can escalate demand and interest.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Info_RealLife: e-Filing inbox notices, refund holds, scrutiny questionnaires.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra maps notice sections to plain-language meaning and checklists.
Tool_income_tax_notice_Info_Motto: Read the section first — then respond with documents.
Tool_tds_on_salary_LearnText: Employers deduct TDS on salary under Section 192. Enter annual gross, regime, and deductions to estimate annual tax and a simple monthly TDS (tax ÷ 12).
Tool_tds_on_salary_FormulaNote: Educational. Real payroll may use projected income, exemptions, and Form 12BB — confirm with your employer / Form 16.
Tool_tds_on_salary_DeductionsHint: 80C, 80D, HRA, etc. Ignored under new regime.
Tool_tds_on_salary_ScenarioSubtitle: New vs old vs 20% higher gross
Tool_tds_on_salary_ExampleStep_3: New-regime slabs + 87A if eligible → annual tax.
Tool_tds_on_salary_ExampleStep_5: Compare old regime in scenario cards.
Tool_tds_on_salary_Info_Purpose: Estimate monthly salary TDS from annual tax liability.
Tool_tds_on_salary_Info_WhyKnow: Helps check Form 16 monthly deductions vs expected tax.
Tool_tds_on_salary_Info_RealLife: Job offers, payroll planning, old vs new regime choice.
Tool_tds_on_salary_Info_Alt_Tax_When: Full regime comparison with more inputs.
Tool_tds_on_salary_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra turns annual tax into a simple Section 192 monthly TDS estimate.
Tool_tds_on_salary_Info_Motto: Monthly TDS is just annual tax spread over the year.
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_LearnText: Under reverse charge, the recipient pays GST. Enter taxable value and rate to see RCM liability, split as CGST+SGST (intra) or IGST (inter).
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_FormulaNote: Educational. RCM applicability (GTA, legal, director fees, etc.) depends on notifications — confirm with your GST practitioner.
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_Result_ItcNote: ITC may be claimable if eligible
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_VerdictNote: Pay {0} under RCM ({1}). Claim ITC only if eligible and compliant.
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_Info_Purpose: Estimate GST payable by the recipient under reverse charge.
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_Info_WhyKnow: RCM shifts cash GST to the recipient even when the supplier does not charge GST.
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_Info_RealLife: Legal fees, GTA, director remuneration, notified services.
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_Info_Differentia: FinYatra shows RCM liability with clear CGST/SGST vs IGST split.
Tool_gst_reverse_charge_Info_Motto: Under RCM, you pay the GST — then check ITC eligibility.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_LearnText: APY locks a guaranteed pension for a fixed monthly contribution by entry age. Put the same monthly amount into NPS and compare guaranteed APY pension vs market-linked NPS pension estimate.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_FormulaNote: Educational. APY is guaranteed; NPS returns and annuity rates vary. Eligibility and tax rules differ — confirm with NPS/APY intermediaries.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_VerdictNote: Same monthly {0}: APY guarantees {1}/mo vs NPS illustrative ~{2}/mo (plus NPS lump).
Tool_apy_vs_nps_ExampleStep_1: Age 30, APY pension ₹5,000 → chart monthly contribution.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_ExampleStep_2: Invest the same monthly amount in NPS to age 60.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_ExampleStep_4: NPS: corpus → 40% annuity → illustrative monthly pension + lump.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_Step_1: Enter entry age and APY pension.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_Step_2: Read APY monthly from the chart.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_Step_3: Run same SIP in NPS to 60.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_Info_Purpose: Compare guaranteed APY pension with NPS for the same monthly outlay.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_Info_WhyKnow: APY is guaranteed but capped; NPS can grow more but is market-linked.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_Info_RealLife: Choosing APY, NPS Tier-I, or a combo for retirement income.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra equalises monthly outlay so APY and NPS are compared fairly.
Tool_apy_vs_nps_Info_Motto: Same rupee in — guaranteed APY vs market-linked NPS.
Tool_property_registration_LearnText: Buying property means stamp duty plus registration charges on the consideration (or guideline value). Use a state preset or enter your own stamp% and registration%.
Tool_property_registration_FormulaNote: Educational. Rates change by state, gender concessions, and local rules — verify with the sub-registrar.
Tool_property_registration_ScenarioSubti: Women vs general vs higher value
Tool_property_registration_ExampleStep_2: Apply stamp % and registration % from preset.
Tool_property_registration_ExampleStep_3: Stamp = value × stamp%; reg = value × reg%.
Tool_property_registration_Info_Purpose: Estimate stamp duty and registration charges on a property purchase.
Tool_property_registration_Info_WhyKnow: These charges are a large cash outlay beyond the sale price.
Tool_property_registration_Info_RealLife: Home purchase budgeting, comparing states, women concession checks.
Tool_property_registration_Info_Differen: FinYatra supports both state presets and custom stamp/reg percentages.
Tool_property_registration_Info_Motto: Budget stamp and registration before you sign.
Tool_mudra_loan_LearnText: PMMY MUDRA loans are capped by category: Shishu up to ₹50,000, Kishore up to ₹5 lakh, Tarun up to ₹10 lakh. Enter amount, rate, and tenure to see EMI on the eligible (capped) amount.
Tool_mudra_loan_FormulaNote: Bank rates and eligibility vary. Collateral-free under PMMY is illustrative — confirm with your lender.
Tool_mudra_loan_CappedNote: Requested {0} exceeds category limit {1} — EMI uses the capped amount.
Tool_mudra_loan_ScenarioSubtitle: Same request under Shishu vs your pick vs Tarun
Tool_mudra_loan_ExampleStep_5: Compare Shishu (capped at ₹50K) and Tarun in scenario cards.
Tool_mudra_loan_Step_2: Enter loan amount, rate, and months.
Tool_mudra_loan_Info_Purpose: Estimate EMI within Shishu / Kishore / Tarun limits.
Tool_mudra_loan_Info_WhyKnow: Category caps decide how much you can borrow under MUDRA.
Tool_mudra_loan_Info_RealLife: Micro / small business working capital and equipment loans.
Tool_mudra_loan_Info_Alt_Emi_When: For any loan without MUDRA caps.
Tool_mudra_loan_Info_Alt_Home_When: For housing finance, not micro business.
Tool_mudra_loan_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra applies Shishu / Kishore / Tarun caps before EMI.
Tool_mudra_loan_Info_Motto: Know your MUDRA category before you plan the EMI.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_LearnText: E-invoicing is generally mandatory for B2B when aggregate turnover crosses the notified threshold (commonly ₹5 crore). Enter turnover and a sample invoice to see GST and a rough mandate flag.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_FormulaNote: Thresholds and penalties change by notification. Educational only — not a compliance filing tool.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_TurnoverHint: Previous FY aggregate turnover (illustrative ₹5 Cr threshold).
Tool_gst_e_invoice_VerdictYes: Turnover suggests e-invoice may apply. Sample GST on this invoice ≈ {0}.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_VerdictNo: Turnover is below the illustrative ₹5 Cr threshold. Sample GST ≈ {0}.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_ScenarioSubtitle: Below vs your turnover vs well above threshold
Tool_gst_e_invoice_ExampleStep_2: Flag: e-invoice likely required for B2B.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_ExampleStep_5: Confirm current CBIC threshold before compliance.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Step_3: Enter sample taxable value and GST %.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Step_4: See sample GST and invoice total.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Info_Purpose: Educational check of e-invoice likelihood plus sample invoice tax.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Info_WhyKnow: Missing e-invoice when mandated can block IRN and attract penalties.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Info_RealLife: GST-registered B2B sellers approaching the turnover threshold.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Info_Alt_Gst_When: For inclusive/exclusive GST on any amount.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Info_Alt_Tax_When: For net GST payable after ITC.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra pairs the ₹5 Cr-style threshold with a sample invoice GST.
Tool_gst_e_invoice_Info_Motto: Know if e-invoice applies before you raise the next B2B bill.
Tool_nri_home_loan_LearnText: NRI home loans often price a bit higher than resident loans. Enter INR income, existing EMIs, requested loan, rate, tenure, and FOIR to see max eligible loan and EMI on the sanctioned estimate.
Tool_nri_home_loan_FormulaNote: FEMA, NRE/NRO repayment, and bank FOIR rules vary. Educational only — not a sanction letter.
Tool_nri_home_loan_IncomeHint: Convert foreign income to INR at a realistic rate.
Tool_nri_home_loan_VerdictOver: Requested {0} exceeds max ≈ {1} — reduce loan or raise income / tenure.
Tool_nri_home_loan_ScenarioSubtitle: Lower rate vs base vs higher income
Tool_nri_home_loan_ExampleStep_3: Max loan from EMI at 9.5% for 20 years.
Tool_nri_home_loan_ExampleStep_4: Sanctioned = min(requested, max loan); compute EMI.
Tool_nri_home_loan_ExampleStep_5: Compare lower rate and higher income scenarios.
Tool_nri_home_loan_Step_1: Enter INR income and existing EMIs.
Tool_nri_home_loan_Step_3: Derive max loan from eligible EMI.
Tool_nri_home_loan_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much home loan an NRI income can support.
Tool_nri_home_loan_Info_WhyKnow: Banks apply FOIR and often higher NRI rates.
Tool_nri_home_loan_Info_RealLife: NRIs buying property in India with NRE/NRO repayment.
Tool_nri_home_loan_Info_Alt_Emi_When: When loan amount is already fixed.
Tool_nri_home_loan_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra combines NRI-style rates with FOIR eligibility and EMI.
Tool_property_capital_gains_LearnText: For property, holding 24+ months is typically long-term. Enter buy and sell prices, holding months, and any Sec 54/54EC exemption to estimate taxable gain and tax at the simplified LTCG rate.
Tool_property_capital_gains_FormulaNote: Post-Budget simplified 12.5% without indexation for many cases. Indexation choice and inherited-property rules need a CA — educational only.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Result_Below: Below 24 months — shown as LTCG path for reference
Tool_property_capital_gains_ScenarioSubt: No exemption vs your input vs higher exemption
Tool_property_capital_gains_ExampleStep_: Try exemption scenarios to see tax drop.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Purpose: Estimate long-term capital gains tax on property sale.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_WhyKnow: Property LTCG and exemptions drive net sale proceeds.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_RealLif: Selling a house or plot and planning Sec 54 reinvestment.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Alt_Cg_: For equity, gold, or debt MF too.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Alt_Hom: For rental NAV, not sale CG.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Differe: FinYatra wraps property LTCG with optional 54/54EC in one flow.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Motto: Sale price is not take-home — check LTCG first.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_LearnText: Form 26AS shows TDS credited against your PAN. Enter TDS from 26AS and from your books (or Form 16) to see if they match and by how much they differ.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_FormulaNot: Also cross-check AIS/TIS. Missing credit may need deductor follow-up. Educational reconciliation only.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_BooksHint: Sum of TDS from salary, interest, contractors, etc. in your records.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_VerdictMat: 26AS and books match — good to proceed with ITR credit checks.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Verdict26a: 26AS shows {0} more than books — verify if books under-recorded TDS.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_VerdictBoo: Books show {0} more than 26AS — follow up with deductors / AIS for missing credit.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_ScenarioSu: Perfect match vs your figures vs books higher
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_ExampleSte: Check if books missed a Form 16A / interest TDS.
Align books or claim the higher 26AS credit carefully.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_Purpo: Spot gaps between Form 26AS credits and your books.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_WhyKn: ITR refunds and demand notices often start with TDS mismatches.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_RealL: Year-end ITR prep and AIS vs Form 16 checks.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_Alt_T: To estimate TDS on a payment.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_Diffe: FinYatra shows 26AS vs books difference in one glance.
Tool_e_shram_LearnText: e-Shram registers unorganised workers and links them to social-security schemes. Toggle registration and schemes to see illustrative annual premiums, accident/life cover, and PM-SYM pension note.
Tool_e_shram_FormulaNote: Premiums and covers are illustrative and change by scheme year. Not an enrolment tool — use official portals.
Tool_e_shram_RegisteredHint: Benefits shown only when registered is Yes.
Tool_e_shram_VerdictNo: Register on e-Shram to unlock linked social-security pathways (educational).
Tool_e_shram_ScenarioSubtitle: Not registered vs your picks vs full suite
Tool_e_shram_ExampleStep_1: Registered = Yes; all three schemes on.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Purpose: Educational snapshot of schemes linked to e-Shram registration.
Tool_e_shram_Info_WhyKnow: Unorganised workers can access low-cost life, accident, and pension schemes.
Tool_e_shram_Info_RealLife: Gig, construction, and informal-sector workers checking benefits.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Alt_Budget_When: To fit premiums into cash flow.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Alt_Term_When: For larger life cover than PMJJBY.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra summarises e-Shram-linked scheme premiums and covers together.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Motto: Register first — then pick the schemes that fit.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_LearnText: EPF often includes an employer share and a higher rate; PPF is voluntary with a ₹1.5L annual cap. Enter the same monthly contribution and years to compare maturity side by side.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_FormulaNote: EPF employee 12% implies basic = contribution / 0.12 when employer match is on. PPF capped at ₹1.5L/year. Educational projection.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_MonthlyHint: Employee EPF share (12% of basic) or PPF monthly equivalent.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_ScenarioSubtitle: No employer match vs base vs longer tenure
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_2: EPF with employer 3.67% at ~8.25% for 15 years.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_5: Toggle employer match off to see employee-only EPF.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_Purpose: Side-by-side maturity for the same contribution habit.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_WhyKnow: Employer match and rate differences can dominate long-term corpus.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_RealLife: Salaried savers choosing extra PPF vs relying on EPF.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra compares EPF (with optional employer match) against PPF on the same monthly habit.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_Motto: Same rupee in — see which corpus wins.
Tool_startup_tax_LearnText: Enter business profit, DPIIT eligibility, and your slab. Eligible DPIIT startups get an illustrative 100% 80-IAC deduction for the year; otherwise tax is profit × slab% + 4% cess. Angel tax on share premium is noted as abolished for DPIIT startups.
Tool_startup_tax_FormulaNote: Educational one-year view. Real 80-IAC has year limits, incorporation conditions, and turnover caps — confirm with your CA.
Tool_startup_tax_DpiitHint: Simplified: if Yes, 100% of profit is deducted under 80-IAC for this year.
Tool_startup_tax_Result_SavedFootnote: Compared with same profit at your slab
Tool_startup_tax_Angel_Abolished: For DPIIT-recognised startups, angel tax on share premium is treated as abolished in this educational view.
Tool_startup_tax_Angel_Na: Without DPIIT recognition, angel-tax rules may still apply on certain share issues — this tool does not compute angel tax.
Tool_startup_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: No DPIIT vs your inputs vs half profit
Tool_startup_tax_ExampleSubtitle: 80-IAC → zero tax in this simplified year
Tool_startup_tax_ExampleStep_5: Angel tax note: abolished for DPIIT in this model.
Tool_startup_tax_Step_3: Apply 100% deduction or tax on profit.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate tax on startup profit with an illustrative DPIIT 80-IAC deduction.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_WhyKnow: DPIIT recognition can wipe out tax on eligible profits for limited years.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_RealLife: Planning tax for a DPIIT-recognised startup’s first profitable years.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_Goal_2: Note angel tax status for DPIIT
Tool_startup_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra pairs DPIIT 80-IAC with a simple profit tax and angel-tax note.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_Motto: Know what DPIIT recognition can save before you plan the year.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_LearnText: Enter last drawn Basic+DA for gratuity, monthly basic for EPF, years of service, and EPF rate. Compare Act-style gratuity with a projected EPF balance — educational, not a retirement plan.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_FormulaNote: Gratuity uses (Basic+DA)×15×years/26. EPF compounds employee+employer EPF share. EPS is not added to corpus.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_SalaryHint: Used in the Payment of Gratuity Act formula.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_BasicHint: PF wages for contribution projection (on actual basic).
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_ScenarioSubtitle: Shorter tenure vs base vs +5 years
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_ExampleStep_3: EPF grows monthly contributions over 20 years to a much larger corpus.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_ExampleStep_5: Both are retirement benefits — compare, don’t substitute one for the other.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Purpose: Compare projected gratuity lump sum with EPF corpus for the same service years.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_WhyKnow: They grow differently — linear formula vs compounding contributions.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_RealLife: Planning exit benefits after long service in a covered establishment.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Goal_1: See gratuity vs EPF side by side
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Goal_2: Understand the gap as tenure grows
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Alt_Gratuity_W: Only gratuity amount and tax exemption.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra shows gratuity and EPF for the same tenure in one view.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Motto: See both exit benefits before you plan retirement cash.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_LearnText: Enter interest income, other IFOS amounts, and your slab. Optionally apply 80TTA (₹10k) or 80TTB (₹50k seniors) on interest. Tax ≈ taxable IFOS × slab% + 4% cess.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_FormulaNo: Simplified IFOS view. Lottery/winnings rates, gift exemptions, and family-pension deductions are not fully modelled.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_OtherHint: Taxable gifts, family pension, etc. (simplified total).
Tool_income_from_other_sources_ScenarioS: No deduction vs base vs higher slab
Tool_income_from_other_sources_ExampleSt: Seniors can use 80TTB up to ₹50,000 instead.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_Purp: Estimate tax on interest and other IFOS at your slab.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_WhyK: IFOS is often overlooked until TDS or ITR time.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_Real: FD interest, dividends already taxed elsewhere, taxable gifts, family pension.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_Diff: FinYatra taxes interest + other IFOS at slab with optional 80TTA/B.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_Mott: Price your other income before ITR season.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_LearnText: Enter the same annual contribution (capped at PPF ₹1.5L), years, NPS expected return, and PPF rate. See side-by-side maturity and the gap. NPS is market-linked; PPF is government-backed — educational only.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_FormulaNote: NPS uses monthly SIP FV; PPF uses annual compounding. Annuity lock-in and tax differences are not fully modelled.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_AnnualHint: Capped at ₹1,50,000 for a fair PPF compare.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_ScenarioSubtitle: Half contribution vs base vs +5 years
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_4: Gap = NPS − PPF (usually favours NPS at higher return).
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_5: PPF is EEE and locked; NPS has annuity rules — compare risk too.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_Purpose: Compare maturity for the same annual outlay in NPS and PPF.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_WhyKnow: Higher NPS return comes with market risk; PPF is steadier.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_RealLife: Choosing how to split ₹1.5L between PPF and NPS.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_Goal_3: Test half contribution or longer tenure
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra compares the same annual rupee in NPS and PPF side by side.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_Motto: Match the same outlay before you pick the vehicle.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_LearnText: Enter the foreign remittance under LRS. This simplified model uses 5% if amount ≤ ₹7 lakh and 20% if above. Education/medical exceptions and Budget rate changes are not fully modelled — confirm with your bank.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_FormulaNo: Illustrative LRS TCS. Thresholds and purpose-based rates change — verify with authorised dealer / CA.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_AmountHin: Total remittance under Liberalised Remittance Scheme.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Status_No: Enter a remittance amount to see TCS.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_ScenarioS: At/below ₹7L vs your amount vs higher
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_ExampleSt: TCS is usually adjustable as tax credit in ITR.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Step_4: See TCS and net cash needed.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Abbr: TCS — Tax collected on LRS remittance
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Purp: Estimate TCS when sending money abroad under LRS.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_WhyK: Banks collect TCS upfront — it affects how much cash you need.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Real: Foreign education fees, travel, or overseas investments under LRS.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Alt_: Tax deducted by payer, not collected on remittance.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Diff: FinYatra focuses LRS remittance on the 5%/20% TCS split.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Mott: Budget the TCS before you remit abroad.
Tool_rental_yield_LearnText: Enter property value, monthly rent, and annual expenses (tax, maintenance, vacancy). See gross yield (rent/value) and net yield after expenses. Typical city yields often sit around 2–5% — educational only.
Tool_rental_yield_FormulaNote: Does not model appreciation, leverage, or house-property tax. Compare with other assets carefully.
Tool_rental_yield_ExpensesHint: Property tax, maintenance, insurance, vacancy allowance.
Tool_rental_yield_ExampleStep_5: Compare with FD/equity yields and tax on rental income separately.
Tool_rental_yield_Step_2: Enter monthly rent and annual expenses.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Abbreviation: Rental yield — income return on property
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Purpose: Measure gross and net rental yield on an investment property.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_WhyKnow: Yield shows income return separate from price appreciation.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_RealLife: Comparing a rental flat with FD or equity MF income.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Alt_Fd_When: Compare income yield with bank FD.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows gross and net rental yield from rent, value, and expenses.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Motto: Know the income yield before you buy for rent.
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_LearnText: Enter Basic+DA, employee and employer NPS, other 80C used, and your slab. Old regime unlocks 80CCD(1) within ₹1.5L and extra ₹50K under (1B). Employer NPS under 80CCD(2) works in both regimes (10% private / 14% central).
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_FormulaNote: Educational. Confirm limits and regime rules with your CA. Cess 4% applied on tax saved.
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_Other80cHint: PPF, ELSS, life insurance, etc. — reduces room for 80CCD(1).

Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_LearnText: If you pay rent but get no HRA, Section 80GG (old regime) allows a deduction equal to the least of ₹60,000/year, 25% of adjusted total income, or rent minus 10% of ATI.

Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Info_Differenti: FinYatra shows all three 80GG limits and the binding one clearly.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Info_Motto: No HRA? Check 80GG before you skip the rent claim.

Tool_debt_payoff_LearnText: Enter balance, rate, EMI, and optional extra payment. We simulate month-by-month payoff and show months saved and interest saved vs EMI alone. For one loan, snowball and avalanche are the same math.

Tool_debt_payoff_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows months and interest saved from extra payments on one loan.

Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Subtitle: SGB കൂപ്പൺ vs ETF നികുതിക്ക് ശേഷം
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_LearnText: Invest the same amount in SGB and Gold ETF. SGB earns 2.5% p.a. on issue price plus gold appreciation (maturity gain tax-free for individuals in this model). ETF tracks gold minus expense ratio and illustrative 12.5% LTCG on gains.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_FormulaNote: Educational. SGB early exit, spreads, and ETF tracking error are ignored. Confirm tax rules for your case.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_YearsHint: SGB tenure is typically 8 years to maturity.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_InterestNote: SGB interest over tenure ≈ {0}. Illustrative ETF LTCG ≈ {1}.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_ScenarioSubtitle: Flat gold vs your view vs +20% end price

Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Step_4: Compute ETF net after expense and tax.

Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Info_WhyKnow: SGB’s 2.5% coupon and tax treatment often beat plain gold ETF over 8 years.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Info_RealLife: Choosing between RBI SGB issue and a gold ETF SIP/lump sum.

Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Info_Motto: Same gold bet — different cash flows and tax.
Tool_fd_ladder_LearnText: Divide your amount equally across N FDs with tenures 1y, 2y, 3y… (or your shortest + step). See each rung’s maturity, total interest, and weighted-average maturity for liquidity planning.

Tool_notice_period_buyout_LearnText: Enter annual CTC and remaining notice days. Buyout cost ≈ days × (CTC / 365). Use it to weigh joining a new role sooner versus serving notice.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_FormulaNote: Companies may use calendar days, working days, or gross monthly ÷ 30. This is an educational estimate — check your offer letter.

Tool_dtaa_LearnText: Under DTAA / Section 90-style foreign tax credit, India usually allows credit up to the Indian tax on the same income. Excess foreign tax is typically not refundable here.

Tool_dtaa_Step_2: Enter illustrative India tax on the same income.

Tool_dtaa_Info_Motto: Credit stops at Indian tax — plan treaties early.

Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_Info_Motto: Know the 80DDB cap before you claim.

Tool_gst_hsn_code_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra uses category presets with CGST/SGST/IGST split.
Tool_gst_hsn_code_Info_Motto: Pick the rate band, then verify the official HSN.
Tool_rent_increase_LearnText: Enter current monthly rent, expected annual increase %, and years to see final monthly rent and total rent paid over the period.
Tool_rent_increase_FormulaNote: Actual increases depend on agreement and local rules. Educational projection only.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_Purpose: Project escalating rent and total outflow.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_WhyKnow: Small % increases compound into large totals.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_RealLife: Lease renewals and long-stay rent planning.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows final rent and cumulative paid together.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_Motto: See where rent goes before you renew.
Tool_rent_increase_ExampleStep_4: Sum annual rents for total paid.
Tool_rent_increase_ExampleStep_5: Compare lower and higher increase scenarios.
Tool_rent_increase_Step_4: Sum total rent paid over the period.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_LearnText: Enter Equity (E), Corporate (C), and Government (G) percentages plus expected returns. The tool checks that weights sum to ~100% and computes a weighted expected return.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_FormulaNote: Age-linked equity caps and Auto choice rules are not fully modelled. Educational only.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_Info_Purpose: Validate weights and blend expected return.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_Info_Different: FinYatra validates 100% and shows blend instantly.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_Info_Motto: Weights that add to 100 — then see the blend.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_InvalidNote: Current sum is {0}. Adjust E/C/G so they add to 100%.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_ScenarioSubtit: Conservative vs your mix vs aggressive
Tool_health_insurance_port_LearnText: Portability can carry forward waiting-period credit already served. Enter original waiting months, months completed, and current vs new sum insured to see waiting left and any cover increase (gap) note.
Tool_health_insurance_port_FormulaNote: IRDAI rules and insurer practices vary. Educational only — not a porting application.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_Purpose: Estimate waiting left and cover-gap note on port.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_WhyKnow: Porting without checking waiting credit can surprise claims.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_RealLife: Switching insurers while keeping PED waiting credit.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_Differen: FinYatra shows leftover waiting and SI gap together.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_Motto: Know waiting left before you port.
Tool_health_insurance_port_ExampleStep_5: Same-SI port keeps waiting credit without gap.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Step_1: Enter original waiting and months completed.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Step_3: Enter current and new sum insured.
Tool_health_insurance_port_GapNote: Cover increase of {0} may restart waiting on the incremental amount — confirm with the new insurer.
Tool_health_insurance_port_ScenarioSubti: Earlier port vs base vs same SI
Tool_variable_pay_tax_LearnText: Enter fixed pay, variable/bonus, and marginal slab. See tax with and without variable (plus cess) and the extra tax attributable to variable pay.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_FormulaNote: Simplified flat slab on amounts — not a full progressive computation.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_Purpose: Compare tax with and without variable at slab.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Bonus months can spike TDS unexpectedly.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_RealLife: CTC with performance bonus / variable pay.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra isolates the extra tax on variable.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_Motto: See what variable really costs after tax.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_ExampleStep_3: Tax with variable on ₹15L at same rate.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: No variable vs base vs +50% variable
Tool_swp_tax_LearnText: Enter SWP withdrawal, average cost per unit, current NAV, and holding months. Units redeemed = withdrawal / NAV; cost basis = units × cost; capital gains tax is estimated on the gain.
Tool_swp_tax_FormulaNote: FIFO lots and ₹1.25L LTCG exemption across funds are simplified. Educational only.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_Purpose: Rough CG tax on each SWP redemption.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_WhyKnow: SWP is not tax-free — only the gain portion is taxed.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_RealLife: Monthly SWP from equity mutual funds in retirement.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra converts withdrawal to units and estimates CG tax.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_Motto: Tax the gain, not the whole withdrawal.
Tool_swp_tax_ExampleStep_5: Compare STCG vs longer LTCG scenarios.
Tool_swp_tax_SlabHint: Used mainly for STCG context; equity LTCG uses illustrative CG rates.
Tool_swp_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: STCG vs your holding vs longer LTCG
Home_Tagline: സ്മാർട്ട് ധനകാര്യത്തിലേക്കുള്ള നിങ്ങളുടെ യാത്ര ഇവിടെ നിന്ന് ആരംഭിക്കുന്നു.
